     110              : /* Context info needed for invoking a recursive querytree display routine */
     111              : typedef struct
     112              : {
     113              :         StringInfo      buf;                    /* output buffer to append to */
     114              :         List       *namespaces;         /* List of deparse_namespace nodes */
     115              :         List       *windowClause;       /* Current query level's WINDOW clause */
     116              :         List       *windowTList;        /* targetlist for resolving WINDOW clause */
     117              :         int                     prettyFlags;    /* enabling of pretty-print functions */
     118              :         int                     wrapColumn;             /* max line length, or -1 for no limit */
     119              :         int                     indentLevel;    /* current indent level for pretty-print */
     120              :         bool            varprefix;              /* true to print prefixes on Vars */
     121              :         ParseExprKind special_exprkind; /* set only for exprkinds needing special
     122              :                                                                          * handling */
     123              :         Bitmapset  *appendparents;      /* if not null, map child Vars of these relids
     124              :                                                                  * back to the parent rel */
     125              : } deparse_context;
     126              : 
     127              : /*
     128              :  * Each level of query context around a subtree needs a level of Var namespace.
     129              :  * A Var having varlevelsup=N refers to the N'th item (counting from 0) in
     130              :  * the current context's namespaces list.
     131              :  *
     132              :  * rtable is the list of actual RTEs from the Query or PlannedStmt.
     133              :  * rtable_names holds the alias name to be used for each RTE (either a C
     134              :  * string, or NULL for nameless RTEs such as unnamed joins).
     135              :  * rtable_columns holds the column alias names to be used for each RTE.
     136              :  *
     137              :  * subplans is a list of Plan trees for SubPlans and CTEs (it's only used
     138              :  * in the PlannedStmt case).
     139              :  * ctes is a list of CommonTableExpr nodes (only used in the Query case).
     140              :  * appendrels, if not null (it's only used in the PlannedStmt case), is an
     141              :  * array of AppendRelInfo nodes, indexed by child relid.  We use that to map
     142              :  * child-table Vars to their inheritance parents.
     143              :  *
     144              :  * In some cases we need to make names of merged JOIN USING columns unique
     145              :  * across the whole query, not only per-RTE.  If so, unique_using is true
     146              :  * and using_names is a list of C strings representing names already assigned
     147              :  * to USING columns.
     148              :  *
     149              :  * When deparsing plan trees, there is always just a single item in the
     150              :  * deparse_namespace list (since a plan tree never contains Vars with
     151              :  * varlevelsup > 0).  We store the Plan node that is the immediate
     152              :  * parent of the expression to be deparsed, as well as a list of that
     153              :  * Plan's ancestors.  In addition, we store its outer and inner subplan nodes,
     154              :  * as well as their targetlists, and the index tlist if the current plan node
     155              :  * might contain INDEX_VAR Vars.  (These fields could be derived on-the-fly
     156              :  * from the current Plan node, but it seems notationally clearer to set them
     157              :  * up as separate fields.)
     158              :  */
     159              : typedef struct
     160              : {
     161              :         List       *rtable;                     /* List of RangeTblEntry nodes */
     162              :         List       *rtable_names;       /* Parallel list of names for RTEs */
     163              :         List       *rtable_columns; /* Parallel list of deparse_columns structs */
     164              :         List       *subplans;           /* List of Plan trees for SubPlans */
     165              :         List       *ctes;                       /* List of CommonTableExpr nodes */
     166              :         AppendRelInfo **appendrels; /* Array of AppendRelInfo nodes, or NULL */
     167              :         /* Workspace for column alias assignment: */
     168              :         bool            unique_using;   /* Are we making USING names globally unique */
     169              :         List       *using_names;        /* List of assigned names for USING columns */
     170              :         /* Remaining fields are used only when deparsing a Plan tree: */
     171              :         Plan       *plan;                       /* immediate parent of current expression */
     172              :         List       *ancestors;          /* ancestors of plan */
     173              :         Plan       *outer_plan;         /* outer subnode, or NULL if none */
     174              :         Plan       *inner_plan;         /* inner subnode, or NULL if none */
     175              :         List       *outer_tlist;        /* referent for OUTER_VAR Vars */
     176              :         List       *inner_tlist;        /* referent for INNER_VAR Vars */
     177              :         List       *index_tlist;        /* referent for INDEX_VAR Vars */
     178              : } deparse_namespace;
     179              : 
     180              : /*
     181              :  * Per-relation data about column alias names.
     182              :  *
     183              :  * Selecting aliases is unreasonably complicated because of the need to dump
     184              :  * rules/views whose underlying tables may have had columns added, deleted, or
     185              :  * renamed since the query was parsed.  We must nonetheless print the rule/view
     186              :  * in a form that can be reloaded and will produce the same results as before.
     187              :  *
     188              :  * For each RTE used in the query, we must assign column aliases that are
     189              :  * unique within that RTE.  SQL does not require this of the original query,
     190              :  * but due to factors such as *-expansion we need to be able to uniquely
     191              :  * reference every column in a decompiled query.  As long as we qualify all
     192              :  * column references, per-RTE uniqueness is sufficient for that.
     193              :  *
     194              :  * However, we can't ensure per-column name uniqueness for unnamed join RTEs,
     195              :  * since they just inherit column names from their input RTEs, and we can't
     196              :  * rename the columns at the join level.  Most of the time this isn't an issue
     197              :  * because we don't need to reference the join's output columns as such; we
     198              :  * can reference the input columns instead.  That approach can fail for merged
     199              :  * JOIN USING columns, however, so when we have one of those in an unnamed
     200              :  * join, we have to make that column's alias globally unique across the whole
     201              :  * query to ensure it can be referenced unambiguously.
     202              :  *
     203              :  * Another problem is that a JOIN USING clause requires the columns to be
     204              :  * merged to have the same aliases in both input RTEs, and that no other
     205              :  * columns in those RTEs or their children conflict with the USING names.
     206              :  * To handle that, we do USING-column alias assignment in a recursive
     207              :  * traversal of the query's jointree.  When descending through a JOIN with
     208              :  * USING, we preassign the USING column names to the child columns, overriding
     209              :  * other rules for column alias assignment.  We also mark each RTE with a list
     210              :  * of all USING column names selected for joins containing that RTE, so that
     211              :  * when we assign other columns' aliases later, we can avoid conflicts.
     212              :  *
     213              :  * Another problem is that if a JOIN's input tables have had columns added or
     214              :  * deleted since the query was parsed, we must generate a column alias list
     215              :  * for the join that matches the current set of input columns --- otherwise, a
     216              :  * change in the number of columns in the left input would throw off matching
     217              :  * of aliases to columns of the right input.  Thus, positions in the printable
     218              :  * column alias list are not necessarily one-for-one with varattnos of the
     219              :  * JOIN, so we need a separate new_colnames[] array for printing purposes.
     220              :  */
     221              : typedef struct
     222              : {
     223              :         /*
     224              :          * colnames is an array containing column aliases to use for columns that
     225              :          * existed when the query was parsed.  Dropped columns have NULL entries.
     226              :          * This array can be directly indexed by varattno to get a Var's name.
     227              :          *
     228              :          * Non-NULL entries are guaranteed unique within the RTE, *except* when
     229              :          * this is for an unnamed JOIN RTE.  In that case we merely copy up names
     230              :          * from the two input RTEs.
     231              :          *
     232              :          * During the recursive descent in set_using_names(), forcible assignment
     233              :          * of a child RTE's column name is represented by pre-setting that element
     234              :          * of the child's colnames array.  So at that stage, NULL entries in this
     235              :          * array just mean that no name has been preassigned, not necessarily that
     236              :          * the column is dropped.
     237              :          */
     238              :         int                     num_cols;               /* length of colnames[] array */
     239              :         char      **colnames;           /* array of C strings and NULLs */
     240              : 
     241              :         /*
     242              :          * new_colnames is an array containing column aliases to use for columns
     243              :          * that would exist if the query was re-parsed against the current
     244              :          * definitions of its base tables.  This is what to print as the column
     245              :          * alias list for the RTE.  This array does not include dropped columns,
     246              :          * but it will include columns added since original parsing.  Indexes in
     247              :          * it therefore have little to do with current varattno values.  As above,
     248              :          * entries are unique unless this is for an unnamed JOIN RTE.  (In such an
     249              :          * RTE, we never actually print this array, but we must compute it anyway
     250              :          * for possible use in computing column names of upper joins.) The
     251              :          * parallel array is_new_col marks which of these columns are new since
     252              :          * original parsing.  Entries with is_new_col false must match the
     253              :          * non-NULL colnames entries one-for-one.
     254              :          */
     255              :         int                     num_new_cols;   /* length of new_colnames[] array */
     256              :         char      **new_colnames;       /* array of C strings */
     257              :         bool       *is_new_col;         /* array of bool flags */
     258              : 
     259              :         /* This flag tells whether we should actually print a column alias list */
     260              :         bool            printaliases;
     261              : 
     262              :         /* This list has all names used as USING names in joins above this RTE */
     263              :         List       *parentUsing;        /* names assigned to parent merged columns */
     264              : 
     265              :         /*
     266              :          * If this struct is for a JOIN RTE, we fill these fields during the
     267              :          * set_using_names() pass to describe its relationship to its child RTEs.
     268              :          *
     269              :          * leftattnos and rightattnos are arrays with one entry per existing
     270              :          * output column of the join (hence, indexable by join varattno).  For a
     271              :          * simple reference to a column of the left child, leftattnos[i] is the
     272              :          * child RTE's attno and rightattnos[i] is zero; and conversely for a
     273              :          * column of the right child.  But for merged columns produced by JOIN
     274              :          * USING/NATURAL JOIN, both leftattnos[i] and rightattnos[i] are nonzero.
     275              :          * Note that a simple reference might be to a child RTE column that's been
     276              :          * dropped; but that's OK since the column could not be used in the query.
     277              :          *
     278              :          * If it's a JOIN USING, usingNames holds the alias names selected for the
     279              :          * merged columns (these might be different from the original USING list,
     280              :          * if we had to modify names to achieve uniqueness).
     281              :          */
     282              :         int                     leftrti;                /* rangetable index of left child */
     283              :         int                     rightrti;               /* rangetable index of right child */
     284              :         int                *leftattnos;         /* left-child varattnos of join cols, or 0 */
     285              :         int                *rightattnos;        /* right-child varattnos of join cols, or 0 */
     286              :         List       *usingNames;         /* names assigned to merged columns */
     287              : } deparse_columns;
     288              : 
     289              : /* This macro is analogous to rt_fetch(), but for deparse_columns structs */
     290              : #define deparse_columns_fetch(rangetable_index, dpns) \
     291              :         ((deparse_columns *) list_nth((dpns)->rtable_columns, (rangetable_index)-1))
     292              : 
     293              : /*
     294              :  * Entry in set_rtable_names' hash table
     295              :  */
     296              : typedef struct
     297              : {
     298              :         char            name[NAMEDATALEN];      /* Hash key --- must be first */
     299              :         int                     counter;                /* Largest addition used so far for name */
     300              : } NameHashEntry;

    2374              : 
    2375              : /* ----------
    2376              :  * pg_get_expr                  - Decompile an expression tree
    2377              :  *
    2378              :  * Input: an expression tree in nodeToString form, and a relation OID
    2379              :  *
    2380              :  * Output: reverse-listed expression
    2381              :  *
    2382              :  * Currently, the expression can only refer to a single relation, namely
    2383              :  * the one specified by the second parameter.  This is sufficient for
    2384              :  * partial indexes, column default expressions, etc.  We also support
    2385              :  * Var-free expressions, for which the OID can be InvalidOid.
    2386              :  *
    2387              :  * If the OID is nonzero but not actually valid, don't throw an error,
    2388              :  * just return NULL.  This is a bit questionable, but it's what we've
    2389              :  * done historically, and it can help avoid unwanted failures when
    2390              :  * examining catalog entries for just-deleted relations.
    2391              :  * ----------
    2392              :  */

    2399              :         int                     prettyFlags;
    2400              : 
    2401            0 :         prettyFlags = PRETTYFLAG_INDENT;
    2402              : 
    2403            0 :         result = pg_get_expr_worker(expr, relid, prettyFlags);
    2404            0 :         if (result)
    2405            0 :                 PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
    2406              :         else
    2407            0 :                 PG_RETURN_NULL();
    2408              : }
    2409              : 
    2410              : Datum
    2411            0 : pg_get_expr_ext(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)

    2415            0 :         bool            pretty = PG_GETARG_BOOL(2);
    2416              :         text       *result;
    2417              :         int                     prettyFlags;
    2418              : 
    2419            0 :         prettyFlags = pretty ? (PRETTYFLAG_PAREN | PRETTYFLAG_INDENT | PRETTYFLAG_SCHEMA) : PRETTYFLAG_INDENT;
    2420              : 
    2421            0 :         result = pg_get_expr_worker(expr, relid, prettyFlags);
    2422            0 :         if (result)
    2423            0 :                 PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);

    2428              : static text *
    2429            0 : pg_get_expr_worker(text *expr, Oid relid, int prettyFlags)
    2430              : {
    2431              :         Node       *node;
    2432              :         List       *context;
    2433              :         char       *exprstr;
    2434            0 :         Relation        rel = NULL;

    2436              : 
    2437              :         /* Convert input TEXT object to C string */

    2439              : 
    2440              :         /* Convert expression to node tree */

    2442              : 
    2443            0 :         pfree(exprstr);
    2444              : 
    2445              :         /*

    2450              :          */

    2452              :         {
    2453            0 :                 rel = try_relation_open(relid, AccessShareLock);
    2454            0 :                 if (rel == NULL)
    2455            0 :                         return NULL;
    2456            0 :                 context = deparse_context_for(RelationGetRelationName(rel), relid);

    2460              :

    2508              : /*

    2542              :                                                 column, tablerv->relname)));
    2543              : 
    2544              :         /* Search the dependency table for the dependent sequence */

    2546              : 
    2547            0 :         ScanKeyInit(&key[0],

    2566              : 
    2567              :                 /*

    2594              : 
    2595            0 :         PG_RETURN_NULL();

    3237              : /* ----------
    3238              :  * deparse_expression_pretty    - General utility for deparsing expressions

    3242              :  *

    3244              :  * for interpreting Vars in the node tree.  It can be NIL if no Vars are
    3245              :  * expected.
    3246              :  *
    3247              :  * forceprefix is true to force all Vars to be prefixed with their table names.
    3248              :  *
    3249              :  * showimplicit is true to force all implicit casts to be shown explicitly.
    3250              :  *
    3251              :  * Tries to pretty up the output according to prettyFlags and startIndent.
    3252              :  *
    3253              :  * The result is a palloc'd string.

    3255              :  */

    3257           19 : deparse_expression_pretty(Node *expr, List *dpcontext,

    3267           19 :         context.windowClause = NIL;
    3268           19 :         context.windowTList = NIL;
    3269           19 :         context.varprefix = forceprefix;
    3270           19 :         context.prettyFlags = prettyFlags;
    3271           19 :         context.wrapColumn = WRAP_COLUMN_DEFAULT;
    3272           19 :         context.indentLevel = startIndent;
    3273           19 :         context.special_exprkind = EXPR_KIND_NONE;
    3274           19 :         context.appendparents = NULL;
    3275              : 
    3276           19 :         get_rule_expr(expr, &context, showimplicit);
    3277              : 
    3278           19 :         return buf.data;
    3279              : }

    3321              : /*
    3322              :  * deparse_context_for_plan_tree - Build deparse context for a Plan tree
    3323              :  *
    3324              :  * When deparsing an expression in a Plan tree, we use the plan's rangetable
    3325              :  * to resolve names of simple Vars.  The initialization of column names for
    3326              :  * this is rather expensive if the rangetable is large, and it'll be the same
    3327              :  * for every expression in the Plan tree; so we do it just once and re-use
    3328              :  * the result of this function for each expression.  (Note that the result
    3329              :  * is not usable until set_deparse_context_plan() is applied to it.)

    3378              : /*
    3379              :  * set_deparse_context_plan - Specify Plan node containing expression
    3380              :  *
    3381              :  * When deparsing an expression in a Plan tree, we might have to resolve
    3382              :  * OUTER_VAR, INNER_VAR, or INDEX_VAR references.  To do this, the caller must
    3383              :  * provide the parent Plan node.  Then OUTER_VAR and INNER_VAR references
    3384              :  * can be resolved by drilling down into the left and right child plans.
    3385              :  * Similarly, INDEX_VAR references can be resolved by reference to the
    3386              :  * indextlist given in a parent IndexOnlyScan node, or to the scan tlist in
    3387              :  * ForeignScan and CustomScan nodes.  (Note that we don't currently support
    3388              :  * deparsing of indexquals in regular IndexScan or BitmapIndexScan nodes;
    3389              :  * for those, we can only deparse the indexqualorig fields, which won't
    3390              :  * contain INDEX_VAR Vars.)
    3391              :  *
    3392              :  * The ancestors list is a list of the Plan's parent Plan and SubPlan nodes,
    3393              :  * the most-closely-nested first.  This is needed to resolve PARAM_EXEC
    3394              :  * Params.  Note we assume that all the Plan nodes share the same rtable.
    3395              :  *
    3396              :  * Once this function has been called, deparse_expression() can be called on
    3397              :  * subsidiary expression(s) of the specified Plan node.  To deparse
    3398              :  * expressions of a different Plan node in the same Plan tree, re-call this
    3399              :  * function to identify the new parent Plan node.
    3400              :  *
    3401              :  * The result is the same List passed in; this is a notational convenience.

    3410           18 :         dpns = (deparse_namespace *) linitial(dpcontext);
    3411              : 
    3412              :         /* Set our attention on the specific plan node passed in */
    3413           18 :         set_deparse_plan(dpns, plan);
    3414           18 :         dpns->ancestors = ancestors;
    3415              : 
    3416           18 :         return dpcontext;
    3417              : }

    3419              : /*
    3420              :  * select_rtable_names_for_explain      - Select RTE aliases for EXPLAIN

    3441              : 
    3442              : /*
    3443              :  * set_rtable_names: select RTE aliases to be used in printing a query
    3444              :  *
    3445              :  * We fill in dpns->rtable_names with a list of names that is one-for-one with
    3446              :  * the already-filled dpns->rtable list.  Each RTE name is unique among those
    3447              :  * in the new namespace plus any ancestor namespaces listed in
    3448              :  * parent_namespaces.
    3449              :  *
    3450              :  * If rels_used isn't NULL, only RTE indexes listed in it are given aliases.
    3451              :  *
    3452              :  * Note that this function is only concerned with relation names, not column

    3454              :  */

    3464              :         ListCell   *lc;
    3465              : 
    3466           22 :         dpns->rtable_names = NIL;

    3469            1 :                 return;
    3470              : 
    3471              :         /*
    3472              :          * We use a hash table to hold known names, so that this process is O(N)
    3473              :          * not O(N^2) for N names.
    3474              :          */
    3475          294 :         MemSet(&hash_ctl, 0, sizeof(hash_ctl));
    3476           21 :         hash_ctl.keysize = NAMEDATALEN;
    3477           21 :         hash_ctl.entrysize = sizeof(NameHashEntry);
    3478           21 :         hash_ctl.hcxt = CurrentMemoryContext;
    3479           21 :         names_hash = hash_create("set_rtable_names names",
    3480           21 :                                                          list_length(dpns->rtable),
    3481              :                                                          &hash_ctl,
    3482              :                                                          HASH_ELEM | HASH_CONTEXT);
    3483              :         /* Preload the hash table with names appearing in parent_namespaces */

    3494            0 :                                 continue;

    3692              : /*

    3701              :  * there is none.  (Forcibly assigning an alias to the join is not a solution,

    3843              :                 /*

    4452              : static char *
    4453           74 : make_colname_unique(char *colname, deparse_namespace *dpns,

    4457              :          * If the selected name isn't unique, append digits to make it so.  For a

    4467              :                 do
    4468              :                 {

    4470              :                         for (;;)

    4483              :                                                                                   colnamelen - 1);
    4484              :                         }
    4485            6 :                 } while (!colname_is_unique(modname, dpns, colinfo));
    4486            6 :                 colname = modname;
    4487              :         }
    4488           74 :         return colname;
    4489              : }

    4491              : /*

    4583              : /*

    4597              : /*
    4598              :  * set_deparse_plan: set up deparse_namespace to parse subexpressions
    4599              :  * of a given Plan node
    4600              :  *
    4601              :  * This sets the plan, outer_plan, inner_plan, outer_tlist, inner_tlist,
    4602              :  * and index_tlist fields.  Caller is responsible for adjusting the ancestors
    4603              :  * list if necessary.  Note that the rtable, subplans, and ctes fields do
    4604              :  * not need to change when shifting attention to different plan nodes in a

    4607              : static void
    4608           42 : set_deparse_plan(deparse_namespace *dpns, Plan *plan)
    4609              : {
    4610           42 :         dpns->plan = plan;
    4611              : 
    4612              :         /*
    4613              :          * We special-case Append and MergeAppend to pretend that the first child
    4614              :          * plan is the OUTER referent; we have to interpret OUTER Vars in their
    4615              :          * tlists according to one of the children, and the first one is the most
    4616              :          * natural choice.  Likewise special-case ModifyTable to pretend that the
    4617              :          * first child plan is the OUTER referent; this is to support RETURNING
    4618              :          * lists containing references to non-target relations.
    4619              :          */
    4620           42 :         if (IsA(plan, Append))
    4621            0 :                 dpns->outer_plan = linitial(((Append *) plan)->appendplans);

    4623            0 :                 dpns->outer_plan = linitial(((MergeAppend *) plan)->mergeplans);
    4624           42 :         else if (IsA(plan, ModifyTable))
    4625            0 :                 dpns->outer_plan = linitial(((ModifyTable *) plan)->plans);
    4626              :         else
    4627           42 :                 dpns->outer_plan = outerPlan(plan);
    4628              : 
    4629           42 :         if (dpns->outer_plan)
    4630           19 :                 dpns->outer_tlist = dpns->outer_plan->targetlist;
    4631              :         else
    4632           23 :                 dpns->outer_tlist = NIL;
    4633              : 
    4634              :         /*
    4635              :          * For a SubqueryScan, pretend the subplan is INNER referent.  (We don't
    4636              :          * use OUTER because that could someday conflict with the normal meaning.)
    4637              :          * Likewise, for a CteScan, pretend the subquery's plan is INNER referent.
    4638              :          * For ON CONFLICT .. UPDATE we just need the inner tlist to point to the
    4639              :          * excluded expression's tlist. (Similar to the SubqueryScan we don't want
    4640              :          * to reuse OUTER, it's used for RETURNING in some modify table cases,
    4641              :          * although not INSERT .. CONFLICT).

    4647            0 :                                                                         ((CteScan *) plan)->ctePlanId - 1);
    4648           42 :         else if (IsA(plan, ModifyTable))

    4650              :         else
    4651           42 :                 dpns->inner_plan = innerPlan(plan);
    4652              : 
    4653           42 :         if (IsA(plan, ModifyTable))

    4671              : /*
    4672              :  * push_child_plan: temporarily transfer deparsing attention to a child plan
    4673              :  *
    4674              :  * When expanding an OUTER_VAR or INNER_VAR reference, we must adjust the
    4675              :  * deparse context in case the referenced expression itself uses
    4676              :  * OUTER_VAR/INNER_VAR.  We modify the top stack entry in-place to avoid
    4677              :  * affecting levelsup issues (although in a Plan tree there really shouldn't
    4678              :  * be any).

    4681              :  * previous state for pop_child_plan.

    4691           23 :         dpns->ancestors = lcons(dpns->plan, dpns->ancestors);
    4692              : 
    4693              :         /* Set attention on selected child */
    4694           23 :         set_deparse_plan(dpns, plan);
    4695           23 : }
    4696              : 
    4697              : /*
    4698              :  * pop_child_plan: undo the effects of push_child_plan

    4711              :         /* Make sure dpns->ancestors is right (may be unnecessary) */
    4712           23 :         dpns->ancestors = ancestors;

    4715              : /*

    4749              :  * pop_ancestor_plan: undo the effects of push_ancestor_plan

    5028              : /* ----------
    5029              :  * get_query_def                        - Parse back one query parsetree
    5030              :  *
    5031              :  * query: parsetree to be displayed

    5034              :  * resultDesc: if not NULL, the output tuple descriptor for the view
    5035              :  *              represented by a SELECT query.  We use the column names from it
    5036              :  *              to label SELECT output columns, in preference to names in the query
    5037              :  * colNamesVisible: true if the surrounding context cares about the output
    5038              :  *              column names at all (as, for example, an EXISTS() context does not);
    5039              :  *              when false, we can suppress dummy column labels such as "?column?"
    5040              :  * prettyFlags: bitmask of PRETTYFLAG_XXX options
    5041              :  * wrapColumn: maximum line length, or -1 to disable wrapping
    5042              :  * startIndent: initial indentation amount

    5044              :  */

    5055           11 :         check_stack_depth();

    5057              :         /*

    5061              :          * querytree!

    5066           11 :         AcquireRewriteLocks(query, false, false);

    5380              : /*
    5381              :  * Detect whether query looks like SELECT ... FROM VALUES(),
    5382              :  * with no need to rename the output columns of the VALUES RTE.
    5383              :  * If so, return the VALUES RTE.  Otherwise return NULL.
    5384              :  */
    5385              : static RangeTblEntry *
    5386           10 : get_simple_values_rte(Query *query, TupleDesc resultDesc)
    5387              : {
    5388           10 :         RangeTblEntry *result = NULL;
    5389              :         ListCell   *lc;
    5390              : 
    5391              :         /*
    5392              :          * We want to detect a match even if the Query also contains OLD or NEW
    5393              :          * rule RTEs.  So the idea is to scan the rtable and see if there is only
    5394              :          * one inFromCl RTE that is a VALUES RTE.
    5395              :          */
    5396           25 :         foreach(lc, query->rtable)
    5397              :         {
    5398           21 :                 RangeTblEntry *rte = (RangeTblEntry *) lfirst(lc);
    5399              : 
    5400           21 :                 if (rte->rtekind == RTE_VALUES && rte->inFromCl)
    5401              :                 {
    5402            3 :                         if (result)
    5403            6 :                                 return NULL;    /* multiple VALUES (probably not possible) */
    5404            3 :                         result = rte;
    5405              :                 }
    5406           18 :                 else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION && !rte->inFromCl)
    5407           12 :                         continue;                       /* ignore rule entries */
    5408              :                 else
    5409            6 :                         return NULL;            /* something else -> not simple VALUES */
    5410              :         }
    5411              : 
    5412              :         /*
    5413              :          * We don't need to check the targetlist in any great detail, because
    5414              :          * parser/analyze.c will never generate a "bare" VALUES RTE --- they only
    5415              :          * appear inside auto-generated sub-queries with very restricted
    5416              :          * structure.  However, DefineView might have modified the tlist by
    5417              :          * injecting new column aliases, or we might have some other column
    5418              :          * aliases forced by a resultDesc.  We can only simplify if the RTE's
    5419              :          * column names match the names that get_target_list() would select.
    5420              :          */
    5421            4 :         if (result)
    5422              :         {
    5423              :                 ListCell   *lcn;
    5424              :                 int                     colno;
    5425              : 
    5426            3 :                 if (list_length(query->targetList) != list_length(result->eref->colnames))
    5427            0 :                         return NULL;            /* this probably cannot happen */
    5428            3 :                 colno = 0;
    5429            7 :                 forboth(lc, query->targetList, lcn, result->eref->colnames)
    5430              :                 {
    5431            4 :                         TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
    5432            4 :                         char       *cname = strVal(lfirst(lcn));
    5433              :                         char       *colname;
    5434              : 
    5435            4 :                         if (tle->resjunk)
    5436            0 :                                 return NULL;    /* this probably cannot happen */
    5437              : 
    5438              :                         /* compute name that get_target_list would use for column */
    5439            4 :                         colno++;
    5440            4 :                         if (resultDesc && colno <= resultDesc->natts)
    5441            2 :                                 colname = NameStr(TupleDescAttr(resultDesc, colno - 1)->attname);
    5442              :                         else
    5443            2 :                                 colname = tle->resname;
    5444              : 
    5445              :                         /* does it match the VALUES RTE? */

    6842              :         /*

    6924              : /*

    6946              : 
    6947              : /*

    7042              : 
    7043              : /*
    7044              :  * Get the name of a field of an expression of composite type.  The
    7045              :  * expression is usually a Var, but we handle other cases too.
    7046              :  *
    7047              :  * levelsup is an extra offset to interpret the Var's varlevelsup correctly.
    7048              :  *
    7049              :  * This is fairly straightforward when the expression has a named composite
    7050              :  * type; we need only look up the type in the catalogs.  However, the type
    7051              :  * could also be RECORD.  Since no actual table or view column is allowed to
    7052              :  * have type RECORD, a Var of type RECORD must refer to a JOIN or FUNCTION RTE
    7053              :  * or to a subquery output.  We drill down to find the ultimate defining
    7054              :  * expression and attempt to infer the field name from it.  We ereport if we
    7055              :  * can't determine the name.
    7056              :  *
    7057              :  * Similarly, a PARAM of type RECORD has to refer to some expression of
    7058              :  * a determinable composite type.
    7059              :  */

    7061            0 : get_name_for_var_field(Var *var, int fieldno,
    7062              :                                            int levelsup, deparse_context *context)

    7064              :         RangeTblEntry *rte;
    7065              :         AttrNumber      attnum;
    7066              :         int                     netlevelsup;
    7067              :         deparse_namespace *dpns;
    7068              :         Index           varno;
    7069              :         AttrNumber      varattno;
    7070              :         TupleDesc       tupleDesc;
    7071              :         Node       *expr;
    7072              : 
    7073              :         /*
    7074              :          * If it's a RowExpr that was expanded from a whole-row Var, use the
    7075              :          * column names attached to it.  (We could let get_expr_result_tupdesc()
    7076              :          * handle this, but it's much cheaper to just pull out the name we need.)
    7077              :          */
    7078            0 :         if (IsA(var, RowExpr))
    7079              :         {
    7080            0 :                 RowExpr    *r = (RowExpr *) var;
    7081              : 
    7082            0 :                 if (fieldno > 0 && fieldno <= list_length(r->colnames))
    7083            0 :                         return strVal(list_nth(r->colnames, fieldno - 1));
    7084              :         }
    7085              : 
    7086              :         /*
    7087              :          * If it's a Param of type RECORD, try to find what the Param refers to.
    7088              :          */
    7089            0 :         if (IsA(var, Param))
    7090              :         {
    7091            0 :                 Param      *param = (Param *) var;
    7092              :                 ListCell   *ancestor_cell;
    7093              : 
    7094            0 :                 expr = find_param_referent(param, context, &dpns, &ancestor_cell);
    7095            0 :                 if (expr)
    7096              :                 {
    7097              :                         /* Found a match, so recurse to decipher the field name */

    7101            0 :                         push_ancestor_plan(dpns, ancestor_cell, &save_dpns);

    7103              :                                                                                         0, context);

    7478              : /*
    7479              :  * Try to find the referenced expression for a PARAM_EXEC Param that might
    7480              :  * reference a parameter supplied by an upper NestLoop or SubPlan plan node.
    7481              :  *

    7483              :  * appropriately for calling push_ancestor_plan().  If no referent can be

    7485              :  */
    7486              : static Node *
    7487            1 : find_param_referent(Param *param, deparse_context *context,

    7489              : {
    7490              :         /* Initialize output parameters to prevent compiler warnings */
    7491            1 :         *dpns_p = NULL;
    7492            1 :         *ancestor_cell_p = NULL;
    7493              : 
    7494              :         /*
    7495              :          * If it's a PARAM_EXEC parameter, look for a matching NestLoopParam or
    7496              :          * SubPlan argument.  This will necessarily be in some ancestor of the
    7497              :          * current expression's Plan node.
    7498              :          */
    7499            1 :         if (param->paramkind == PARAM_EXEC)
    7500              :         {
    7501              :                 deparse_namespace *dpns;
    7502              :                 Plan       *child_plan;
    7503              :                 bool            in_same_plan_level;
    7504              :                 ListCell   *lc;
    7505              : 
    7506            1 :                 dpns = (deparse_namespace *) linitial(context->namespaces);
    7507            1 :                 child_plan = dpns->plan;
    7508            1 :                 in_same_plan_level = true;
    7509              : 
    7510            1 :                 foreach(lc, dpns->ancestors)
    7511              :                 {
    7512            1 :                         Node       *ancestor = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
    7513              :                         ListCell   *lc2;
    7514              : 
    7515              :                         /*
    7516              :                          * NestLoops transmit params to their inner child only; also, once
    7517              :                          * we've crawled up out of a subplan, this couldn't possibly be
    7518              :                          * the right match.
    7519              :                          */
    7520            1 :                         if (IsA(ancestor, NestLoop) &&
    7521            0 :                                 child_plan == innerPlan(ancestor) &&
    7522              :                                 in_same_plan_level)
    7523              :                         {
    7524            0 :                                 NestLoop   *nl = (NestLoop *) ancestor;
    7525              : 
    7526            0 :                                 foreach(lc2, nl->nestParams)
    7527              :                                 {
    7528            0 :                                         NestLoopParam *nlp = (NestLoopParam *) lfirst(lc2);
    7529              : 
    7530            0 :                                         if (nlp->paramno == param->paramid)
    7531              :                                         {
    7532              :                                                 /* Found a match, so return it */
    7533            0 :                                                 *dpns_p = dpns;
    7534            0 :                                                 *ancestor_cell_p = lc;
    7535            0 :                                                 return (Node *) nlp->paramval;
    7536              :                                         }
    7537              :                                 }
    7538              :                         }
    7539              : 
    7540              :                         /*
    7541              :                          * If ancestor is a SubPlan, check the arguments it provides.
    7542              :                          */
    7543            1 :                         if (IsA(ancestor, SubPlan))
    7544            0 :                         {
    7545            1 :                                 SubPlan    *subplan = (SubPlan *) ancestor;
    7546              :                                 ListCell   *lc3;
    7547              :                                 ListCell   *lc4;
    7548              : 
    7549            1 :                                 forboth(lc3, subplan->parParam, lc4, subplan->args)
    7550              :                                 {
    7551            1 :                                         int                     paramid = lfirst_int(lc3);
    7552            1 :                                         Node       *arg = (Node *) lfirst(lc4);
    7553              : 
    7554            1 :                                         if (paramid == param->paramid)
    7555              :                                         {
    7556              :                                                 /*
    7557              :                                                  * Found a match, so return it.  But, since Vars in
    7558              :                                                  * the arg are to be evaluated in the surrounding
    7559              :                                                  * context, we have to point to the next ancestor item
    7560              :                                                  * that is *not* a SubPlan.
    7561              :                                                  */
    7562              :                                                 ListCell   *rest;
    7563              : 
    7564            1 :                                                 for_each_cell(rest, dpns->ancestors,
    7565              :                                                                           lnext(dpns->ancestors, lc))
    7566              :                                                 {
    7567            1 :                                                         Node       *ancestor2 = (Node *) lfirst(rest);
    7568              : 
    7569            1 :                                                         if (!IsA(ancestor2, SubPlan))
    7570              :                                                         {
    7571            1 :                                                                 *dpns_p = dpns;
    7572            1 :                                                                 *ancestor_cell_p = rest;
    7573            1 :                                                                 return arg;
    7574              :                                                         }
    7575              :                                                 }
    7576            0 :                                                 elog(ERROR, "SubPlan cannot be outermost ancestor");
    7577              :                                         }
    7578              :                                 }
    7579              : 
    7580              :                                 /* We have emerged from a subplan. */
    7581            0 :                                 in_same_plan_level = false;
    7582              : 
    7583              :                                 /* SubPlan isn't a kind of Plan, so skip the rest */
    7584            0 :                                 continue;
    7585              :                         }
    7586              : 
    7587              :                         /*
    7588              :                          * Check to see if we're emerging from an initplan of the current
    7589              :                          * ancestor plan.  Initplans never have any parParams, so no need
    7590              :                          * to search that list, but we need to know if we should reset

    7592              :                          */
    7593            0 :                         foreach(lc2, ((Plan *) ancestor)->initPlan)
    7594              :                         {
    7595            0 :                                 SubPlan    *subplan = castNode(SubPlan, lfirst(lc2));
    7596              : 
    7597            0 :                                 if (child_plan != (Plan *) list_nth(dpns->subplans,
    7598            0 :                                                                                                         subplan->plan_id - 1))
    7599            0 :                                         continue;
    7600              : 
    7601              :                                 /* No parameters to be had here. */
    7602              :                                 Assert(subplan->parParam == NIL);
    7603              : 
    7604              :                                 /* We have emerged from an initplan. */
    7605            0 :                                 in_same_plan_level = false;
    7606            0 :                                 break;
    7607              :                         }
    7608              : 
    7609              :                         /* No luck, crawl up to next ancestor */
    7610            0 :                         child_plan = (Plan *) ancestor;
    7611              :                 }
    7612              :         }
    7613              : 
    7614              :         /* No referent found */
    7615            0 :         return NULL;
    7616              : }

    7618              : /*
    7619              :  * Display a Param appropriately.
    7620              :  */
    7621              : static void

    7623              : {
    7624              :         Node       *expr;
    7625              :         deparse_namespace *dpns;
    7626              :         ListCell   *ancestor_cell;

    7628              :         /*

    7640              :                 bool            need_paren;
    7641              : 
    7642              :                 /* Switch attention to the ancestor plan node */
    7643            1 :                 push_ancestor_plan(dpns, ancestor_cell, &save_dpns);
    7644              : 
    7645              :                 /*
    7646              :                  * Force prefixing of Vars, since they won't belong to the relation

    7651              :

    7663              : 
    7664            1 :                 get_rule_expr(expr, context, false);
    7665              : 
    7666            1 :                 if (need_paren)

    7668              : 
    7669            1 :                 context->varprefix = save_varprefix;
    7670              : 
    7671            1 :                 pop_ancestor_plan(dpns, &save_dpns);
    7672              : 
    7673            1 :                 return;
    7674              :         }

    7708              : /*

    7726              :                 case T_SetToDefault:

    7730              : 
    7731            0 :                 case T_SubscriptingRef:
    7732              :                 case T_ArrayExpr:
    7733              :                 case T_RowExpr:
    7734              :                 case T_CoalesceExpr:

    7736              :                 case T_SQLValueFunction:

    7745            0 :                         return true;
    7746              : 
    7747              :                         /* CASE keywords act as parentheses */
    7748            0 :                 case T_CaseExpr:

    7773            0 :                 case T_CoerceViaIO:

    7776            0 :                 case T_ArrayCoerceExpr:

    7798              : 
    7799              :                                         /* We know only the basic operators + - and * / % */

    7924              : 
    7925              : /*
    7926              :  * appendContextKeyword - append a keyword to buffer
    7927              :  *
    7928              :  * If prettyPrint is enabled, perform a line break, and adjust indentation.
    7929              :  * Otherwise, just append the keyword.
    7930              :  */
    7931              : static void
    7932            9 : appendContextKeyword(deparse_context *context, const char *str,
    7933              :                                          int indentBefore, int indentAfter, int indentPlus)
    7934              : {
    7935            9 :         StringInfo      buf = context->buf;
    7936              : 
    7937            9 :         if (PRETTY_INDENT(context))
    7938              :         {
    7939              :                 int                     indentAmount;
    7940              : 
    7941            9 :                 context->indentLevel += indentBefore;
    7942              : 
    7943              :                 /* remove any trailing spaces currently in the buffer ... */
    7944            9 :                 removeStringInfoSpaces(buf);
    7945              :                 /* ... then add a newline and some spaces */
    7946            9 :                 appendStringInfoChar(buf, '\n');
    7947              : 
    7948            9 :                 if (context->indentLevel < PRETTYINDENT_LIMIT)
    7949            9 :                         indentAmount = Max(context->indentLevel, 0) + indentPlus;
    7950              :                 else
    7951              :                 {
    7952              :                         /*
    7953              :                          * If we're indented more than PRETTYINDENT_LIMIT characters, try
    7954              :                          * to conserve horizontal space by reducing the per-level
    7955              :                          * indentation.  For best results the scale factor here should
    7956              :                          * divide all the indent amounts that get added to indentLevel
    7957              :                          * (PRETTYINDENT_STD, etc).  It's important that the indentation
    7958              :                          * not grow unboundedly, else deeply-nested trees use O(N^2)
    7959              :                          * whitespace; so we also wrap modulo PRETTYINDENT_LIMIT.
    7960              :                          */
    7961            0 :                         indentAmount = PRETTYINDENT_LIMIT +
    7962            0 :                                 (context->indentLevel - PRETTYINDENT_LIMIT) /
    7963              :                                 (PRETTYINDENT_STD / 2);
    7964            0 :                         indentAmount %= PRETTYINDENT_LIMIT;
    7965              :                         /* scale/wrap logic affects indentLevel, but not indentPlus */
    7966            0 :                         indentAmount += indentPlus;
    7967              :                 }
    7968            9 :                 appendStringInfoSpaces(buf, indentAmount);
    7969              : 
    7970            9 :                 appendStringInfoString(buf, str);
    7971              : 
    7972            9 :                 context->indentLevel += indentAfter;
    7973            9 :                 if (context->indentLevel < 0)
    7974            0 :                         context->indentLevel = 0;
    7975              :         }
    7976              :         else
    7977            0 :                 appendStringInfoString(buf, str);
    7978            9 : }
    7979              : 
    7980              : /*
    7981              :  * removeStringInfoSpaces - delete trailing spaces from a buffer.
    7982              :  *
    7983              :  * Possibly this should move to stringinfo.c at some point.
    7984              :  */
    7985              : static void
    7986            9 : removeStringInfoSpaces(StringInfo str)
    7987              : {
    7988           11 :         while (str->len > 0 && str->data[str->len - 1] == ' ')
    7989            2 :                 str->data[--(str->len)] = '\0';
    7990            9 : }

    7992              : 
    7993              : /*

    7999              :  * Any node that does *not* embrace its argument node by sql syntax (with
    8000              :  * parentheses, non-operator keywords like CASE/WHEN/ON, or comma etc) should

    8016           34 :         get_rule_expr(node, context, showimplicit);
    8017              : 
    8018           34 :         if (need_paren)
    8019            0 :                 appendStringInfoChar(context->buf, ')');

    8023              : /* ----------

    8069              :

    8509              :                                 }

    8515            0 :                                         if (caseexpr->arg)

    8521              :                                                  * For accurate decompilation of rules it's essential
    8522              :                                                  * that we show just the RHS.  However in an
    8523              :                                                  * expression that's been through the optimizer, the
    8524              :                                                  * WHEN clause could be almost anything (since the
    8525              :                                                  * equality operator could have been expanded into an

    8533            0 :                                                         if (list_length(args) == 2 &&

    8536            0 :                                                                 w = (Node *) lsecond(args);

    8538              :                                         }
    8539              : 
    8540            0 :                                         if (!PRETTY_INDENT(context))
    8541            0 :                                                 appendStringInfoChar(buf, ' ');

    8543              :                                                                                  0, 0, 0);
    8544            0 :                                         get_rule_expr(w, context, false);
    8545            0 :                                         appendStringInfoString(buf, " THEN ");

    8547              :                                 }

    8551              :                                                                          0, 0, 0);

    8560            0 :                 case T_CaseTestExpr:

    8573            0 :                 case T_ArrayExpr:

    8581              :                                 /*

    8589              :                         }

    8600              :                                 /*

    8605            0 :                                 if (rowexpr->row_typeid != RECORDOID)

    8607            0 :                                         tupdesc = lookup_rowtype_tupdesc(rowexpr->row_typeid, -1);

    8628            0 :                                                 sep = ", ";

    8640            0 :                                                         sep = ", ";
    8641              :                                                 }
    8642            0 :                                                 i++;
    8643              :                                         }
    8644              : 
    8645            0 :                                         ReleaseTupleDesc(tupdesc);
    8646              :                                 }
    8647            0 :                                 appendStringInfoChar(buf, ')');

    8654            0 :                 case T_RowCompareExpr:

    8926              :

    9202              : /*
    9203              :  * get_rule_expr_toplevel               - Parse back a toplevel expression
    9204              :  *
    9205              :  * Same as get_rule_expr(), except that if the expr is just a Var, we pass
    9206              :  * istoplevel = true not false to get_variable().  This causes whole-row Vars
    9207              :  * to get printed with decoration that will prevent expansion of "*".
    9208              :  * We need to use this in contexts such as ROW() and VALUES(), where the
    9209              :  * parser would expand "foo.*" appearing at top level.  (In principle we'd
    9210              :  * use this in get_target_list() too, but that has additional worries about
    9211              :  * whether to print AS, so it needs to invoke get_variable() directly anyway.)
    9212              :  */
    9213              : static void
    9214            9 : get_rule_expr_toplevel(Node *node, deparse_context *context,
    9215              :                                            bool showimplicit)
    9216              : {
    9217            9 :         if (node && IsA(node, Var))
    9218            2 :                 (void) get_variable((Var *) node, 0, true, context);
    9219              :         else
    9220            7 :                 get_rule_expr(node, context, showimplicit);
    9221            9 : }
    9222              : 
    9223              : /*
    9224              :  * get_rule_list_toplevel               - Parse back a list of toplevel expressions

    9240              :         {
    9241            2 :                 Node       *e = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
    9242              : 
    9243            2 :                 appendStringInfoString(context->buf, sep);
    9244            2 :                 get_rule_expr_toplevel(e, context, showimplicit);
    9245            2 :                 sep = ", ";
    9246              :         }
    9247            1 : }

    9249              : /*
    9250              :  * get_rule_expr_funccall               - Parse back a function-call expression
    9251              :  *

    9256              :  * you can't substitute a parenthesized a_expr.  If what we have isn't going
    9257              :  * to look like a function call, wrap it in a dummy CAST() expression, which

    9261              : static void

    9266            0 :                 get_rule_expr(node, context, showimplicit);

    9269            0 :                 StringInfo      buf = context->buf;
    9270              : 
    9271            0 :                 appendStringInfoString(buf, "CAST(");
    9272              :                 /* no point in showing any top-level implicit cast */

    9275              :                                                  format_type_with_typemod(exprType(node),

    9278            0 : }

    9301            0 :                 default:
    9302            0 :                         break;

    9706              : /* ----------

    9868              : /*
    9869              :  * simple_quote_literal - Format a string as a SQL literal, append to buf

    9871              : static void
    9872            2 : simple_quote_literal(StringInfo buf, const char *val)
    9873              : {
    9874              :         const char *valptr;

    9877              :          * We form the string literal according to the prevailing setting of

    9885              : 
    9886            2 :                 if (SQL_STR_DOUBLE(ch, !standard_conforming_strings))
    9887            0 :                         appendStringInfoChar(buf, ch);
    9888            2 :                 appendStringInfoChar(buf, ch);
    9889              :         }
    9890            2 :         appendStringInfoChar(buf, '\'');
    9891            2 : }

    9894              : /* ----------
    9895              :  * get_sublink_expr                     - Parse back a sublink

    9898              : static void
    9899            1 : get_sublink_expr(SubLink *sublink, deparse_context *context)

    9910              : 
    9911              :         /*

    9950            0 :                         appendStringInfoChar(buf, ')');

   10121              : /* ----------
   10122              :  * get_from_clause                      - Parse back a FROM clause
   10123              :  *

   10128              :  */
   10129              : static void
   10130            7 : get_from_clause(Query *query, const char *prefix, deparse_context *context)

   10134              :         ListCell   *l;
   10135              : 
   10136              :         /*
   10137              :          * We use the query's jointree as a guide to what to print.  However, we
   10138              :          * must ignore auto-added RTEs that are marked not inFromCl. (These can
   10139              :          * only appear at the top level of the jointree, so it's sufficient to
   10140              :          * check here.)  This check also ensures we ignore the rule pseudo-RTEs

   10148              :                 {

   10152            6 :                         if (!rte->inFromCl)
   10153            0 :                                 continue;
   10154              :                 }

   10156            6 :                 if (first)

   10158            6 :                         appendContextKeyword(context, prefix,
   10159              :                                                                  -PRETTYINDENT_STD, PRETTYINDENT_STD, 2);
   10160            6 :                         first = false;
   10161              : 
   10162            6 :                         get_from_clause_item(jtnode, query, context);
   10163              :                 }
   10164              :                 else
   10165              :                 {
   10166              :                         StringInfoData itembuf;
   10167              : 
   10168            0 :                         appendStringInfoString(buf, ", ");
   10169              : 
   10170              :                         /*
   10171              :                          * Put the new FROM item's text into itembuf so we can decide
   10172              :                          * after we've got it whether or not it needs to go on a new line.
   10173              :                          */

   10175            0 :                         context->buf = &itembuf;
   10176              : 
   10177            0 :                         get_from_clause_item(jtnode, query, context);
   10178              : 
   10179              :                         /* Restore context's output buffer */
   10180            0 :                         context->buf = buf;
   10181              : 
   10182              :                         /* Consider line-wrapping if enabled */

   10184              :                         {
   10185              :                                 /* Does the new item start with a new line? */

   10190            0 :                                         removeStringInfoSpaces(buf);

   10193              :                                 {
   10194              :                                         char       *trailing_nl;
   10195              : 
   10196              :                                         /* Locate the start of the current line in the buffer */

   10198            0 :                                         if (trailing_nl == NULL)

   10201            0 :                                                 trailing_nl++;

   10203              :                                         /*

   10209              :                                                                                          PRETTYINDENT_STD,

   10211              :                                 }
   10212              :                         }
   10213              : 
   10214              :                         /* Add the new item */

   10874              : /*

   10908              :                         /* okay */

   10911              :                 {
   10912            2 :                         safe = false;

   10917              :

   10920              : 
   10921          956 :         if (safe)

   10958              : /*

   11275              : /*

   11281              :  * rightop are given as strings and are assumed to yield types leftoptype

   11283              :  * comes from needing to be sure that the parser will select the desired
   11284              :  * operator when the query is parsed.  We always name the operator using
   11285              :  * OPERATOR(schema.op) syntax, so as to avoid search-path uncertainties.

   11287              :  * of the operator; else we are at the mercy of the parser's heuristics for
   11288              :  * ambiguous-operator resolution.  The caller must ensure that leftop and
